page_content:
THET420 Language and the Actor (3 Credits)

Explores the actor's relationship to language, particularly heightened poetic language, in order to: develop the ability to embody language

and vocally and physically project the images; apply an intellectual understanding of the inherent structural, poetic, and rhetorical techniques of heightened language in combination with action theory; and access the inner states of character while expressing them through text. Prerequisite: THET325 or THET324; and must audition; and permission of ARHU-School of Theatre, Dance & Performance Studies department.
